16.q: Sod2 is the primary mechanism of salt removal across the plasma membrane of S. pombe. When the sod2 protein is deleted, this yeast is unable to grow in the presence of external Na+ or Li+.
transgenic lines accumulated less Na+ and more K+ in the symplast than the wt plants did

17.q: Loss of functional Pka1 causes the KCl-sensitive phenotypes; cAMP synthesized by Cyr1 binds to Cgs1, thereby releasing Pka1 in an activated form.
The produced cAMP binds to protein kinase A regulatory subunit Cgs1, leading to the release of the catalytic subunit Pka1.

18.q: Our results demonstrate that Pps1 plays an important role in the salt stress response in S. pombe.

19.q: Pyp1 is known to be a main negative regulator of a stress-activated MAPK, Spc1.
